description: DocumentDB Agent-Kit knowledge base. Single source of truth mapping natural-language diagnostic intents to the exact scripts that answer them (one hop), with a schema for multi-hop troubleshooting workflows (guarded diagnostic graph) to be populated later.

conventions.notes: Every tool reads a live DocumentDB container locally (mongosh + psql). Recommendations are measured facts, never generic rules-of-thumb.

tools.title: Index Redundancy Finder
tools.produces: list of redundant/unused/prefix/duplicate indexes with severity and replacement
example_queries: do I have redundant or unused indexes

tools.title: Large-Document / TOAST Advisor
tools.produces: collections whose large text fields are TOASTed and detoasted on every scan, with the schema-split fix
example_queries: why are my aggregations slow even with indexes
is large text hurting my scans

tools.title: Config & Cache Advisor
tools.produces: shared_buffers vs measured working set, TOAST share, cache-hit ratios (evidence-based)
example_queries: what is my working set and cache hit ratio

tools.title: Performance Advisor
tools.produces: overall health: collection/index overview, collection-scan audit, query timing, PG I/O/locks/config
example_queries: give my database a performance checkup

tools.title: Data Integrity Checker
tools.produces: orphaned foreign-key references and field type inconsistencies (hard structural integrity)
example_queries: do I have orphaned or broken references

routes_one_hop.description: Representative NL query -> tool id. Used for testing and as extra routing signal; the router also scores against each tool's keywords/example_queries.

examples.query: my writes got slow after adding indexes
aggregations are slow but I have indexes
my documents have huge text fields
is my cache hit ratio ok
find missing indexes and collection scans

workflow_schema.description: Schema for MULTI-HOP troubleshooting workflows: a guarded diagnostic graph (AND/OR). Each step runs a tool; edges are conditional on the observed result. The LLM agent presents a step, the human runs it (or the agent runs the script), and the reported result selects the next step or a conclusion. This layer is scaffolded; workflows are added over time.

<step_id>.question: what the step establishes
depends_on: step ids that must run first (AND edges)

workflows.title: Diagnose slow writes (SCAFFOLD — illustrative, not yet validated)

check_redundant_indexes.question: Are redundant/unused indexes taxing every write?

findings_gt_0.or_conclude: Redundant/unused indexes are a write tax; drop the flagged indexes.

check_document_bloat.question: Are large co-located text fields making each write rewrite TOAST?

bloat_found.or_conclude: Large co-located text inflates write/TOAST cost; split text into a side collection.

check_config.question: Is the working set exceeding cache, causing write-path I/O?

working_set_over_cache.or_conclude: Working set exceeds shared_buffers; shrink documents or size cache to the measured working set.

fits.or_conclude: No index/bloat/cache cause found at this layer; escalate to workload/lock analysis.
